metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2462
metadata.comment:
Rationale: LRRC4 interacts with netrin-G CAMs through its ectodomain in an isoform-specific manner.

4/3/2018 Pim
- Netrin-G2; NTNG2 is localized at the presynaptic side (see ticket #2322)
- LRRC4 is located at the postsynaptic side (Fig.2; see ticket #2219)
- Adhesion between these proteins is tested in Fig.1C
- Literal: "It is not known whether NGL and netrin-G mediate cell adhesion. L cells transiently expressing NGL-1 and NGL-2 selectively aggregated with cells expressing netrin-G1 and netrin-G2, respectively (Supplementary Fig. 3 online), suggesting that the association between NGL and netrin-G mediates cell adhesion in an isoform-specific manner.". Experimental description: Methods
Immunoelectron microscopy.
For quantitative electron microscopy, we used two immunoreacted sections from each of two rats labeled with secondary antibody to rabbit Fab fragments coupled to 10 nm gold particles (British BioCell).

Coculture assay.
Coculture assays were performed essentially as described (17,18).
(ref. 17) Cell Culture.
Hippocampal neuron cultures were prepared from E18 rat embryos.

DNA constructs.
Full-length mouse NGL-2 (DQ177325, aa 1-652; from a brain cDNA library; Clontech) and NGL-2 ΔC (1-649) were subcloned into pEGFP-N1.
